articles.title: Trump Administration Allows Anthropic to Release Mythos to Select US Organizations
articles.subtitle: After weeks of negotiations, the White House permitted Anthropic to grant access to its most advanced AI model to a select group of US companies and government agencies.
articles.author: Maxwell Zeff
articles.summary: After weeks of negotiations, the White House permitted Anthropic to grant access to its most advanced AI model to a select group of US companies and government agencies.
body: The US government has eased the restrictions it imposed on Anthropic’s most advanced AI model, Claude Mythos 5 , allowing the company to grant access to more than 100 US organizations, including large corporations and government agencies.

In a letter sent to Anthropic’s cofounder and chief compute officer Tom Brown obtained by WIRED, US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick told the AI lab it would permit certain trusted partners to access Mythos because he had “determined that appropriate safeguards are in place.” Semafor first reported the existence of the letter.
“Anthropic has worked with the U.S. government to address risks associated with the Covered Models. These efforts have yielded significant progress,” Lutnick wrote.

However, the government stopped short of permitting a broader rollout of the model, and said nothing about the fate of Claude Fable 5, the consumer-facing version of Mythos that Anthropic released with significant additional safeguards. Lutnick noted in his letter that the other requirements outlined in the initial directive he sent on June 12 remain in effect.

“We received notice from the US government that Mythos 5, our strongest cybersecurity model, can be redeployed to a small group of cyber defenders and infrastructure providers,” Anthropic spokesperson Eduardo Maia Silva said in a statement to WIRED. “We are working to provision the approved set of providers and restore their access to Mythos 5 as quickly as possible. We are pleased to see this progress and continue to work with the government to expand access to Mythos 5 and make Fable 5 available for general use again.”

Anthropic is still in discussions with the White House about restoring access to Fable 5, and they are expected to continue over the weekend, according to a person familiar with the matter. Both parties are hopeful the resolution of this incident will help inform a lasting policy framework for future model releases, the person said.

The partial reinstatement comes roughly two weeks after the White House sent an export control directive to Anthropic that required the company to limit foreign nationals from accessing Mythos and Fable 5, including people working and living in the United States. In response, Anthropic disabled access to the models entirely. In his latest letter, Lutnick wrote that organizations approved to use Mythos may now allow their foreign national employees to access the model, and Anthropic may do the same for its own foreign national employees.

The Trump administration grew concerned about Anthropic’s rollout of Mythos after it learned the company granted access to a South Korean telecommunications firm it believed had ties to China, WIRED previously reported. Amazon and the National Security Agency also separately raised concerns to the White House that Fable 5 could be jailbroken, and the confluence of events convinced officials they needed to take action.

In recent weeks, Anthropic sent senior members from its cybersecurity and AI safety teams to Washington, DC to meet with Trump administration officials. Along with Brown, Anthropic's public policy chief Sarah Heck have been leading the company’s discussions with the US Department of Commerce.

Getting Mythos 5 back online marks a promising step forward for Anthropic and the White House, but the saga has raised broader questions about the overall direction of US AI policy, particularly the extent to which the Trump administration will seek to control future model releases. On Friday, OpenAI announced it was delaying the release of its upcoming GPT 5.6 models in response to a request from the Trump administration.

Dean Ball, head of the strategic futures team at OpenAI and a former White House AI adviser, said in a June 16 blog post that Anthropic’s latest spat with the Trump administration has shown frontier AI model developers they “need an explicit green light from the government now.”

Anthropic’s battles with the White House have been costly for the young company’s business. It sued the Trump administration earlier this year over a supply chain risk designation it received after trying to draw red lines around how military contractors could use its AI models. After the White House sent its export directive on the Friday evening of June 12, WIRED previously reported that some Anthropic investors worked through the weekend to determine what it meant for Anthropic’s corporate future.
keyPoints: The US government has eased the restrictions it imposed on Anthropic’s most advanced AI model, Claude Mythos 5 , allowing the company to grant access to more than 100 US organizations, including large corporations and government agencies.

In a letter sent to Anthropic’s cofounder and chief compute officer Tom Brown obtained by WIRED, US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick told the AI lab it would permit certain trusted partners to access Mythos because he had “determined that appropriate safeguards are in place.” Semafor first reported the existence of the letter.
“Anthropic has worked with the U.S. government to address risks associated with the Covered Models. These efforts have yielded significant progress,” Lutnick wrote.
paragraphTranslations: 美国政府放宽了对Anthropic最先进的人工智能模型Claude Mythos 5的限制，允许该公司向100多个美国组织提供访问权限，包括大型公司和政府机构。
在一封发送给Anthropic联合创始人兼首席计算官汤姆·布朗的信件中，美国商务部长霍华德·卢特尼克告诉这家人工智能实验室，他将允许某些值得信赖的合作伙伴访问Mythos，因为他已经“确定已经建立了适当的安全措施”。Semafor首次报道了这封信的存在。
“Anthropic已经与美国政府合作，解决了与Covered Models相关的风险。这些努力取得了显著进展，”卢特尼克写道。
然而，政府没有允许更广泛地推出该模型，并就Anthropic发布的Mythos的消费版本Claude Fable 5的命运只字未提，该版本拥有额外的重大安全措施。卢特尼克在信中指出，他在6月12日发出的初始指令中概述的其他要求仍然有效。
“我们收到了美国政府的通知，我们的最强网络安全模型Mythos 5可以重新部署到一小群网络防御者和基础设施提供商，”Anthropic发言人爱德华多·迈亚·席尔瓦在一份声明中对WIRED表示。“我们正在努力为获批的服务提供商提供服务，并尽快恢复他们对Mythos 5的访问权限。我们对这一进展感到高兴，并将继续与政府合作，扩大对Mythos 5的访问权限，并再次使Fable 5可供公众使用。”
Anthropic仍在与白宫讨论恢复对Fable 5的访问权限，预计讨论将持续到周末，据一位知情人士透露。双方都希望这一事件的解决将有助于为未来模型发布提供一个持久的政策框架，该人士表示。
大约两周前，白宫向Anthropic发送了一项出口控制指令，要求该公司限制外国公民访问Mythos和Fable 5，包括在美国工作和生活的人。作为回应，Anthropic完全禁用了对这些模型的访问。在最新的信件中，卢特尼克写道，获准使用Mythos的组织现在可以允许他们的外国公民员工访问该模型，Anthropic也可以为其自己的外国公民员工做同样的事情。
特朗普政府在了解到Anthropic向一家被认为与中国有联系的韩国电信公司授予访问权限后，对Mythos的推出表示担忧，WIRED此前曾报道。亚马逊和国家安全局也分别向白宫提出了对Fable 5可能被越狱的担忧，一系列事件的汇合使官员们相信他们需要采取行动。
最近几周，Anthropic派出其网络安全和人工智能安全团队的高级成员前往华盛顿特区，与特朗普政府官员会面。与布朗一起，Anthropic的公共政策主管莎拉·赫克一直在领导公司与美国商务部的讨论。
让Mythos 5重新上线对Anthropic和白宫来说是一个有希望的进展，但这一事件引发了关于美国人工智能政策总体方向的更广泛问题，特别是特朗普政府将在多大程度上寻求控制未来模型的发布。周五，OpenAI宣布，应特朗普政府的请求，推迟发布即将推出的GPT 5.6模型。
OpenAI战略未来团队负责人、前白宫人工智能顾问迪安·鲍尔在6月16日的博客文章中表示，Anthropic与特朗普政府的最新争执表明，前沿人工智能模型开发者现在“需要政府的明确绿灯”。
Anthropic与白宫的斗争对这家年轻公司的业务造成了损失。今年早些时候，它就试图划定军事承包商如何使用其人工智能模型的红线后收到的供应链风险指定，起诉了特朗普政府。在白宫于6月12日周五晚间发出其出口指令后，WIRED此前曾报道，一些Anthropic的投资者在周末工作，以确定这对Anthropic的企业未来意味着什么。

paragraphs: The US government has allowed Anthropic to release its advanced AI model, Claude Mythos 5, to over 100 US organizations including corporations and government agencies. This decision came after US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick confirmed that 'appropriate safeguards are in place'. Anthropic has been working with the US government to mitigate risks associated with the AI model. However, the government has not yet approved the consumer version, Claude Fable 5. Anthropic is in ongoing discussions with the White House to restore access to Fable 5.
paragraphTranslations: 美国政府允许Anthropic向100多个美国组织，包括公司和政府机构，发布其先进的人工智能模型Claude Mythos 5。这一决定是在商务部长霍华德·卢特尼克确认“适当的安全措施已经到位”之后做出的。Anthropic一直在与美国政府合作，以减轻与人工智能模型相关的风险。然而，政府尚未批准消费者版本Claude Fable 5。Anthropic正在与白宫进行持续讨论，以恢复对Fable 5的访问。

articles.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 amid US AI regulatory drama
articles.subtitle: Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. On Friday, the company unveiled the limited preview of its new GPT 5.6 model suit
articles.author: Hayden Field
articles.summary: Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. On Friday, the company unveiled the limited preview of its new GPT 5.6 model suite: Sol, the flagship; Terra, a medium-tier model for "high-volume work"; and Luna, a […]
body: The new release includes three models: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. On Friday, the company unveiled the limited preview of its new GPT 5.6 model suite: Sol, the flagship; Terra, a medium-tier model for “high-volume work”; and Luna, a “fast and affordable” everyday model. OpenAI says it’s especially skilled at coding, cybersecurity, and biology, as well as staying focused during long-horizon agentic AI tasks.

Per million tokens, GPT-5.6 Sol is priced at $5 input / $30 output (nearly half the cost of Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5, which is $10 input / $50 output). Terra is half the cost of Sol, and Luna is less than half the cost of Terra. The company also debuted two additional modes for Sol: a “max” mode for deeper reasoning and an “ultra” mode for leveraging sub-agents — evoking OpenClaw, and perhaps a sign of OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger ’s work at OpenAI so far.

Unsurprisingly amid a security panic in Washington, D.C. , OpenAI dedicated the majority of its announcement blog post to safety and potential misuse. It appeared to reference the recent jailbreaking travails of its rival Anthropic, writing that “GPT‐5.6 is trained to refuse prohibited cyber assistance, including when users attempt to disguise their intent or jailbreak the model.” It also said that flagship model Sol “is better at helping people find and fix vulnerabilities than reliably carrying out end-to-end attacks,” and that Sol doesn’t cross the cyber-critical threshold under OpenAI’s preparedness framework — though it should be noted that OpenAI recently revised its preparedness framework in April and removed some areas of previous study.

The company said Sol has the company’s “most robust safety stack to date” and that it “strengthened protections for higher-risk activity, sensitive cyber requests, and repeated misuse.” OpenAI said it had dedicated “approximately 700,000 A100e GPU hours” to automated red-teaming and also worked with third-party testers, the latter of which will continue to test it for the next two weeks.

OpenAI also seemed to be taking an extra-sensitive approach during the preview period, which is being closely monitored by the Trump administration. The company wrote that “safeguards may occasionally intervene on legitimate work, particularly in dual-use areas where defensive and offensive activity can initially look similar. That is part of what the preview is designed to test.” The report earlier this week said that the Trump administration will approve customers on a case-by-case basis during the preview period.

OpenAI said the model suite should be generally available in the coming weeks because the company believes in “broad access,” and that the company cooperated with the US government ahead of this launch, but that it hopefully wouldn’t be the norm.

“We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” the company wrote. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them. We are taking this short-term step because we believe it is the strongest path to broader availability in the coming weeks, while we work with the Administration to develop the cyber Executive Order framework and a repeatable process for future model releases.”
keyPoints: The new release includes three models: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. On Friday, the company unveiled the limited preview of its new GPT 5.6 model suite: Sol, the flagship; Terra, a medium-tier model for “high-volume work”; and Luna, a “fast and affordable” everyday model. OpenAI says it’s especially skilled at coding, cybersecurity, and biology, as well as staying focused during long-horizon agentic AI tasks.

Per million tokens, GPT-5.6 Sol is priced at $5 input / $30 output (nearly half the cost of Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5, which is $10 input / $50 output). Terra is half the cost of Sol, and Luna is less than half the cost of Terra. The company also debuted two additional modes for Sol: a “max” mode for deeper reasoning and an “ultra” mode for leveraging sub-agents — evoking OpenClaw, and perhaps a sign of OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger ’s work at OpenAI so far.
paragraphTranslations: 新版本包括三个模型：Sol、Terra和Luna。
在新闻爆出OpenAI将应特朗普政府的请求分阶段发布下一代模型后不到24小时，那个模型，GPT-5.6，就在这里。周五，公司公布了其新GPT 5.6模型套件的有限预览：Sol，旗舰产品；Terra，一个用于“大量工作”的中端模型；以及Luna，一个“快速且实惠”的日常模型。OpenAI表示，它特别擅长编码、网络安全和生物学，以及在长期地平线代理AI任务中保持专注。

每百万个标记，GPT-5.6 Sol的价格为输入5美元/输出30美元（几乎是Anthropic的Claude Fable 5成本的一半，后者为输入10美元/输出50美元）。Terra的成本是Sol的一半，而Luna的成本不到Terra的一半。该公司还首次推出了Sol的两种额外模式：一种“max”模式用于更深入的推理，以及一种“ultra”模式用于利用子代理——唤起OpenClaw，也许是OpenClaw创始人Peter Steinberger在OpenAI迄今为止的工作成果。

在华盛顿特区的安全恐慌中，OpenAI不出所料地将其公告博客文章的大部分内容都用于安全和潜在的滥用。它似乎提到了其竞争对手Anthropic最近的越狱困境，写道“GPT-5.6被训练为拒绝禁止的网络援助，包括当用户试图掩饰他们的意图或越狱模型时。”它还表示，旗舰模型Sol“在帮助人们发现和修复漏洞方面比可靠地执行端到端攻击更好”，并且Sol在OpenAI的准备框架下没有越过网络关键阈值——尽管值得注意的是，OpenAI最近在4月份修订了其准备框架，并删除了一些先前研究的领域。
公司表示，Sol拥有公司“迄今为止最强大的安全栈”，并且它“加强了对高风险活动、敏感网络请求和重复滥用的保护。”OpenAI表示，它已经投入了“大约700,000 A100e GPU小时”到自动化红队测试中，并与第三方测试人员合作，后者将继续在未来两周内对其进行测试。
OpenAI在预览期间似乎也在采取额外敏感的方法，这正受到特朗普政府的密切监控。公司写道，“安全措施可能会偶尔介入合法工作，特别是在防御和攻击活动最初看起来相似的双重用途领域。这是预览旨在测试的一部分。”本周早些时候的报告说，特朗普政府将在预览期间逐案批准客户。
OpenAI表示，模型套件应该在未来几周内普遍可用，因为公司相信“广泛访问”，并且公司在这次发布前与美国政府合作，但希望这不会成为常态。
“我们不相信这种政府访问流程应该成为长期默认设置，”公司写道。“它使用户、开发者、企业、网络防御者和全球合作伙伴无法获得最好的工具。我们采取这一短期步骤，是因为我们相信这是未来几周更广泛可用性最强的路径，同时我们与政府合作开发网络行政命令框架和未来模型发布的可重复流程。”

vocabulary.example: The hotel offers a suite of rooms for large families.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 这家酒店为大家庭提供一套房间。

vocabulary.example: Cybersecurity is a major concern for businesses.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 网络安全是企业的主要关切。

paragraphs: OpenAI has released its GPT-5.6 model suite, which includes Sol, Terra, and Luna. Sol is the main model, Terra is for high-volume work, and Luna is an affordable everyday model. These models are good at coding, cybersecurity, and biology. They can also stay focused on long tasks. Sol costs less than a similar model from a rival company. OpenAI has added extra safety features to its models to prevent misuse, especially in cybersecurity. The company worked with the US government before launching the models and will make them widely available soon.
paragraphTranslations: OpenAI 发布了包含 Sol、Terra 和 Luna 的 GPT-5.6 模型套件。Sol 是主要模型，Terra 用于大量工作，Luna 是经济实惠的日常模型。这些模型擅长编码、网络安全和生物学。它们还能专注于长期任务。与竞争对手公司的类似模型相比，Sol 的成本更低。为了防止滥用，OpenAI 在其模型中增加了额外的安全特性，尤其是在网络安全方面。该公司在推出模型前与美国政府合作，并将很快广泛提供这些模型。

The company worked with the US government before launching the models and will make them widely available soon.
paragraphTranslations: OpenAI 发布了包含 Sol、Terra 和 Luna 的 GPT-5.6 模型套件。Sol 是主要模型，Terra 用于大量工作，Luna 是经济实惠的日常模型。这些模型擅长编码、网络安全和生物学。它们还能专注于长期任务。与竞争对手公司的类似模型相比，Sol 的成本更低。为了防止滥用，OpenAI 在其模型中增加了额外的安全特性，尤其是在网络安全方面。该公司在推出模型前与美国政府合作，并将很快广泛提供这些模型。
